No time should be lost in thanking the Lord for his kindness; our praises are most acceptable, pleasant, and profitable when they come from a full heart. This would excite and deepen love and gratitude in the hearts of believers; events would be better known and longer remembered. Whatever Deborah, Barak, or the army had done, the Lord must have all the praise. The will, the power, and the success were all from Him.

Deborah describes Israel’s distressed state under Jabin's tyranny so that their salvation might appear more gracious. She shows what brought this misery upon them: their idolatry. They chose new gods with new names, but under these images, Satan was worshipped. Deborah was a mother to Israel by diligently promoting the salvation of their souls. She calls on those who shared in this great salvation to offer thanks to God for it. Those who are restored, not only to their liberty as other Israelites but also to their rank, should speak God’s praises. This is the Lord's doing. In these acts, justice was executed on his enemies. During times of persecution, God’s ordinances, the walls of salvation from which the waters of life are drawn, are sought even at the risk of lives. At all times Satan will try to hinder the believer from approaching the throne of grace. Notice God's kindness to his trembling people. It is God’s glory to protect those who are most exposed and to help the weakest. Let us notice the benefit we have from public peace, especially for the inhabitants of villages, and give God the praise.

Deborah called on her own soul to be earnest. Whoever wants to set other men's hearts on fire with love for Christ must himself burn with that love. Praising God is a work we should awaken to and awaken ourselves unto. She notices who fought against Israel, who fought for them, and who stayed away. Those who fought against them were obstinate enemies of God’s people, therefore more dangerous. The tribes that helped are spoken of with honor; for though God is above all to be glorified, those who are employed must have their due praise to encourage others. But the whole creation is at war with those who are enemies of God. The river Kishon fought against their enemies. It was usually shallow, but now, probably because of a great rain, it swelled and became deep and strong, so that those who tried to cross were drowned. Deborah’s own soul fought against them. When the soul is engaged in holy exercises, and heart-work is made of them through God's grace, the strength of our spiritual enemies will be trampled down and fall before us. She observes who stayed away and did not side with Israel as might have been expected. Many are kept from doing their duty by fear of trouble, love of ease, and undue affection for their worldly business and advantage. Selfish people do not care what happens to God’s church so long as they can get, keep, and save money. Everyone seeks his own. A little will serve those who have no desire to engage in necessary services because there is difficulty and danger involved. But we cannot stay away from the conflict between the Lord and his enemies; if we do not actively try to promote his cause in this wicked world, we shall fall under the curse against those who work iniquity. Though He needs no human help, he is pleased to accept the services of those who use their talents to advance his cause. He requires every man to do so.

Jael had a special blessing. Those whose lot is cast in a tent, in a low and narrow sphere, if they serve God according to the powers he has given them, will not lose their reward. Sisera’s mother looked for his return, not fearing his success at all. Let us be careful about indulging eager desires toward any temporal good, especially that which fosters vanity; for that is what she here cherished. What a picture she presents of an ungodly and sensual heart! How shameful and childish these wishes are from an aged mother and her attendants regarding her son! And thus God often brings ruin on his enemies when they are most proud. Deborah concludes with a prayer to God for the destruction of all his foes and for the comfort of all his friends. Such will be the honor and joy of all who love God sincerely; they shall shine forever as the sun in the sky.
